2605.29335.title: Rethinking FID Through the Geometry of the Reference Dataset
2605.29335.paper_summary: Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) is widely used to evaluate image generators, yet lower FID does not always correspond to better sample quality. We show that this mismatch depends in part on the geometry of the reference dataset. In a controlled study across six datasets, distributional density and effective rank significantly explain how FID changes as sample quality improves. Concentrated datasets tend to yield more favorable FID trends, whereas more dispersed datasets can make FID worsen despite better samples. Attribution to precision and recall and ablations with alternative feature spaces and distances support the same conclusion. These results suggest that distributional metrics should be interpreted together with the geometry of the reference dataset for more reliable benchmarking.
2605.29335.paper_summary_zh: Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) 被廣泛用來評估圖像生成器，但較低的 FID 並不總是對應於更好的樣本質量。<lb/>我們顯示這種不匹配在某種程度上取決於參考數據集的幾何特性。<lb/>在六個數據集的控制研究中，分佈密度和有效秩顯著解釋了隨著樣本質量提高 FID 如何變化。<lb/>集中型數據集往往會產生更有利的 FID 趨勢，而更分散的數據集則可能使 FID 惡化，儘管樣本質量更好。<lb/>對精確度和召回率的歸因以及使用替代特徵空間和距離的消融實驗支持了相同的結論。<lb/>這些結果表明，分佈度量應與參考數據集的幾何特性一起解釋，以獲得更可靠的基準評估。
2605.29335.author: Yunghee Lee et.al.
